title: Check Axis Position, Reference State, and Motion Status on the Visu_MCP_Dual Screen
summary: Use the Visu_MCP_Dual axis display fields on the operator station HMI to verify left and right gripper axis positions, determine whether each axis is referenced, and identify whether an axis is currently in motion based on the documented field labels and color indicators.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when you need to interpret the Visu_MCP_Dual screen to confirm current A1, Z1, A2, and Z2 axis positions, verify whether each axis has been referenced, and determine whether an axis is in motion. This is appropriate for troubleshooting-oriented observation of tipper axis state on the HMI.
not_for: Do not use this procedure as a corrective action for restoring reference state or clearing faults; the source packet only supports observation and interpretation.
Do not use this procedure to command motion, home axes, or change settings; those actions are not defined in this source-supported candidate.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Open and interpret the Visu_MCP_Dual screen, read axis position values, and assess reference and motion indicators.
Provide access to the operator station HMI or assist with screen navigation if needed.

safety_notes: This candidate is limited to observation and interpretation on the HMI; no corrective actions are supported by the source packet.
An axis being out of reference may cause a tipper fault.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the operator station HMI
Axis position and reference status display

access_required: Access to the operator station HMI
Ability to view the Visu_MCP_Dual screen
system_preconditions: The Tipper Machine Control Panel (Visu_MCP_Dual) screen must be available for viewing.

visual_references.description: Operator Station Control Panel image for locating the Visu_MCP_Dual screen and axis position area.
Operator station screen showing axis labels Z1, A1, Z2, and A2 that may help orient the user to axis naming on the HMI.
Manual jog mode reference showing A1, Z1, A2, and Z2 axis naming conventions for left and right axes.

steps.title: Open the Visu_MCP_Dual screen and locate the axis fields
steps.purpose: Identify the correct HMI screen and the axis display area used for this diagnostic check.
steps.instruction: Open the Tipper Machine Control Panel ("Visu_MCP_Dual") screen on the operator station HMI and locate the A1, Z1, A2, and Z2 boxes in the axis position area.
steps.expected_result: The Visu_MCP_Dual screen is visible and the A1, Z1, A2, and Z2 boxes can be identified.
steps.healthy_condition: All four axis boxes are visible and distinguishable on the screen.
steps.failure_condition: The Visu_MCP_Dual screen cannot be accessed or the axis boxes cannot be confidently identified.

actions.label: Open Visu_MCP_Dual
actions.exact_action: Open the Tipper Machine Control Panel ("Visu_MCP_Dual") screen and locate the A1, Z1, A2, and Z2 boxes.

actions.label: Identify axis fields
actions.exact_action: Locate the A1, Z1, A2, and Z2 boxes in the axis position area of the screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall Visu_MCP_Dual operator station control panel view to orient to the primary screen and axis display area.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the displayed screen does not match the documented Visu_MCP_Dual control panel view.
Escalate if the axis position area cannot be located on the HMI.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 4-19, titled "Operator Station Control Panel," appears on page 47 of the OptiSweep Operation and Maintenance Manual in section 4.1.2.1, Tipper Machine Control Panel (Visu_MCP_Dual screen).

The "A1" and "Z1" boxes show the current position (in steps) on the A-axis and Z-axis of the left gripper. The "A2" and "Z2" boxes show the current position (in steps) on the A-axis and Z-axis of the right gripper.

steps.title: Read left gripper axis positions
steps.purpose: Determine the current displayed position values for the left gripper axes.
steps.instruction: Read A1 and Z1 on the screen as the current position in steps for the left gripper A-axis and Z-axis.
steps.expected_result: The current left gripper A-axis and Z-axis positions are identified from the displayed values.
steps.healthy_condition: A1 and Z1 display readable current position values in steps.
steps.failure_condition: A1 or Z1 is unreadable, missing, or does not appear to represent current position in steps.

actions.label: Read A1 and Z1
actions.exact_action: Read A1 and Z1 as the current position in steps for the left gripper A-axis and Z-axis.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Axis position boxes corresponding to A1 and Z1 for the left gripper.
Axis naming reference showing A1 and Z1 used for the left axis.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if A1 or Z1 cannot be read from the HMI.
Escalate if the left-axis labels do not match the documented A1/Z1 naming.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The "A1" and "Z1" boxes show the current position (in steps) on the A-axis and Z-axis of the left gripper.
The source also notes that A1 and Z1 are used for the left axis, while A2 and Z2 are used for the right tipper.

steps.title: Read right gripper axis positions
steps.purpose: Determine the current displayed position values for the right gripper axes.
steps.instruction: Read A2 and Z2 on the screen as the current position in steps for the right gripper A-axis and Z-axis.
steps.expected_result: The current right gripper A-axis and Z-axis positions are identified from the displayed values.
steps.healthy_condition: A2 and Z2 display readable current position values in steps.
steps.failure_condition: A2 or Z2 is unreadable, missing, or does not appear to represent current position in steps.

actions.label: Read A2 and Z2
actions.exact_action: Read A2 and Z2 as the current position in steps for the right gripper A-axis and Z-axis.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Axis position boxes corresponding to A2 and Z2 for the right gripper.
Axis naming reference showing A2 and Z2 used for the right tipper.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if A2 or Z2 cannot be read from the HMI.
Escalate if the right-axis labels do not match the documented A2/Z2 naming.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The "A2" and "Z2" boxes show the current position (in steps) on the A-axis and Z-axis of the right gripper.
The source also notes that A1 and Z1 are used for the left axis, while A2 and Z2 are used for the right tipper.

steps.title: Check axis reference indicators
steps.purpose: Determine whether each axis has been referenced using the documented indicator.
steps.instruction: Check the "R" indicator for each axis and verify whether the circle is green to confirm the axis has been referenced.
steps.expected_result: Each axis can be classified as referenced or not referenced based on the "R" indicator.
steps.healthy_condition: The circle for an axis is green, indicating that axis has been referenced.
steps.failure_condition: The circle is not green for an axis, indicating the axis is out of reference.

actions.label: Inspect R indicators
actions.exact_action: Check the 'R' indicator for each axis and verify whether the circle is green to confirm the axis has been referenced.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Reference-state indicator area associated with each axis on the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if an axis is not referenced and a fault is present or suspected.
Escalate if the reference indicator behavior does not match the documented green-circle meaning.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The 'R' in the cycle denotes whether or not the axis has been "referenced": if the circle is green, the axis has been referenced.

steps.title: Check whether any axis is in motion
steps.purpose: Determine whether any axis is currently moving using the documented field color behavior.
steps.instruction: Check whether any data field is yellow to determine whether that axis is in motion.
steps.expected_result: Any axis currently in motion is identified by a yellow data field.
steps.healthy_condition: A stationary axis does not show the motion-indicating yellow field; a moving axis shows yellow as documented.
steps.failure_condition: Field color behavior cannot be interpreted or does not match the documented motion indication.

actions.label: Inspect field color for motion
actions.exact_action: Check whether any data field is yellow to determine whether that axis is in motion.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Axis data fields to determine whether any field is yellow, indicating motion.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if field color behavior does not match the documented yellow-means-motion indicator.
Escalate if axis motion status cannot be determined from the HMI.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The data field will be yellow when the axis is in motion.

steps.title: Record out-of-reference axes and note fault relevance
steps.purpose: Document any abnormal reference state and identify when escalation is warranted.
steps.instruction: Record any axis that is not referenced, since the source states an axis being out of reference may cause a tipper fault.
steps.expected_result: Any out-of-reference axis is documented for follow-up or escalation.
steps.healthy_condition: All axes needed for the check are referenced, or any non-referenced axis is clearly documented.
steps.failure_condition: One or more axes are out of reference, especially when associated with a tipper fault.

actions.label: Document out-of-reference axes
actions.exact_action: Record any axis that is not referenced, since the source states an axis being out of reference may cause a tipper fault.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Axis reference indicators and position fields for any axis identified as out of reference.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if an out-of-reference axis is associated with a fault.
Escalate if the displayed axis state does not match documented indicators.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: An axis being "out of reference" may cause a tipper fault.
The 'R' in the cycle denotes whether or not the axis has been "referenced": if the circle is green, the axis has been referenced.

success_criteria: The user identifies the current A1, Z1, A2, and Z2 position values from the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.
The user determines whether each axis is referenced using the green "R" indicator.
The user determines whether any axis is in motion using the yellow data field indication.
Any out-of-reference axis is documented for follow-up.
healthy_conditions: A1, Z1, A2, and Z2 are visible and readable as current position values in steps.
A green circle at the "R" indicator shows an axis has been referenced.
A yellow data field indicates an axis is in motion, consistent with the documented screen behavior.
failure_conditions: The Visu_MCP_Dual screen or axis fields cannot be located or interpreted.
An axis reference indicator does not match the documented meaning.
An axis is out of reference.
Displayed axis state does not match documented indicators.
An out-of-reference axis is associated with a tipper fault.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the displayed axis state does not match documented indicators or if an out-of-reference axis is associated with a fault.
Escalate if the Visu_MCP_Dual screen cannot be accessed or the axis fields cannot be identified.
This runbook does not include source-supported corrective actions; escalate rather than improvise unsupported recovery steps.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Primary source-backed screen view for the Visu_MCP_Dual control panel and axis display area.
Axis naming orientation for Z1, A1, Z2, and A2 on an operator station screen.
Additional axis naming reference for left and right tipper axes.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The "A1" and "Z1" boxes show the current position (in steps) on the A-axis and Z-axis of the left gripper. The "A2" and "Z2" boxes show the current position (in steps) on the A-axis and Z-axis of the right gripper.

The 'R' in the cycle denotes whether or not the axis has been "referenced": if the circle is green, the axis has been referenced. The data field will be yellow when the axis is in motion. An axis being "out of reference" may cause a tipper fault.
